disclaimer: Figures below are methodological summaries and placeholders until snapshot KPIs are wired in. They are not audited accounts. Replace “—” with numbers from your CSV exports or CA working.

questions.title: How much profit have we made so far?
questions.summary: True profit needs a full P&L (CA / Tally P&L), not sales minus purchases alone. You are still adding ECPL salaries, complete expenses, and Jan–Mar purchase bills.
questions.advice: Publish a single kpis.json (or add rows here) once CA P&L is available. Until then, show “gross margin on booked purchases” only as an indicator, never as statutory profit.

questions.title: How much rent is owed to Harshad Mehta (accrual)?
questions.summary: Accrual per your rule: ₹1,00,000 per month from 01-10-2024 through 31-03-2026 inclusive = 18 months → ₹18,00,000 (gross of tax unless you specify otherwise).

figures.label: Accrued rent @ ₹1L/month
figures.note: Verify if any months are exempt or pro-rated.

gaps: Written confirmation if GST on rent is added on top
questions.advice: Add a small rent_schedule.csv (month, accrued, paid_from_ecpl, paid_from_sw) and optionally let the app sum it.

questions.title: How much have we paid Harshad so far (rent)?
questions.summary: Your rule: any direct payment to Harshad after 1 Oct 2024 counts as rent. From ECPL Axis FY 25–26 extract, NEFT lines to NAKUL HARSHAD MEHTA total ₹2,46,500; plus review the ₹2,00,000 cash line naming NAKUL if that is the same payee.

figures.label: ECPL Axis — NEFT to NAKUL HARSHAD MEHTA (six lines)
figures.note: Nov 2025 – Mar 2026 in the PDF-derived CSV.

figures.label: ECPL Axis — cash withdrawal ‘NAKUL ME’
figures.note: Classify as rent only if you confirm it went to Harshad.

figures.label: Space Within — payments to Harshad
figures.note: Sum from SW HDFC after you tag payee rows.

gaps: Full ECPL bank for pre–Apr 2025 if any rent paid earlier
questions.advice: Export a harshad_payments_all_sources.csv with columns: date, amount, bank_account, narration, source_file.

questions.title: What advance / income tax was paid for Harshad Mehta?
questions.summary: Formula you gave: 30% × Space Within profit. Until SW profit is fixed, the tax liability line stays open. Payments should be traced on Space Within HDFC (and any other bank used) by narration/challan.

figures.label: Payments identified as advance/self-assessment tax
figures.note: Filter SW (and other) banks for ITNS/OLTAS/income tax narrations.

gaps: Challan PDFs or tagged payment export
questions.advice: Do not confuse ECPL’s GST payments with Harshad’s personal income tax — keep separate tags in the ledger.

questions.title: How much OD interest did the bank charge (Space Within), and what is pending vs ECPL?
questions.summary: Use actual interest debits on Space Within HDFC from 01-10-2024 to 31-03-2026. A quick pass on FY 25–26 SW data showed interest-related withdrawals on the order of ~₹1.2L+ (needs line-by-line confirmation). ECPL Axis FY 25–26 did not obviously label interest rows in the same way — OD cost sits primarily on SW statements per your setup.

figures.label: SW HDFC — interest-type debits (FY 25–26 sample pass)
figures.note: Re-run after full tagging for Oct’24–Mar’26.

figures.label: SW HDFC — line containing ‘OD’ (sample)
figures.note: Confirm with bank certificate if needed.

figures.label: Pending repayment to Space Within
figures.note: Allocated portion agreed with stakeholders minus any amounts already reimbursed.

gaps: Apr–Sep 2024 interest if you extend window
Agreement on principal vs interest split if repaying SW
questions.advice: Export interest_charges_sw.csv (date, amount, narration, running OD balance if available). Treat as ‘amount to allocate’ until books post it.

questions.title: How much does ECPL owe Nakul and STPL?
questions.summary: Not a single bank filter: ‘owe’ needs opening balances + agreed character of STPL inflows (loan vs revenue) + Nakul reimbursements. STPL→ECPL credits on ECPL Axis alone were about ₹85.7L in one analytical pass — that is cash received, not automatically ‘debt’.

figures.label: STPL credits visible on ECPL Axis (indicative)
ECPL → Nakul / Harshad (direct)
figures.note: Not the same as ‘owed to Nakul’.

questions.advice: Build a simple intercompany_matrix.csv: date, from_party, to_party, amount, type (loan/sale/expense), evidence_file.

questions.title: How much does STPL owe ECPL?
questions.summary: Symmetric to the above: requires agreed treatment of trading balances vs loans. SW→STPL and ECPL→STPL debits exist in parallel; net position is not derivable from one statement.
gaps: Invoices issued to STPL vs payments
questions.advice: Start from **invoices + Tally receivable** for trade, and a separate **loan schedule** for STPL advances.

questions.title: What data is still missing for a clean close?
questions.summary: You already flagged: ECPL purchase bills Jan–Mar; complete ECPL expenses; salaries; CA P&L. Also: unified Harshad payment extract across ECPL + SW; challans for Harshad tax; full OD interest extract for full reconciliation window.
questions.advice: Use the Parties & sources tab as a checklist — flip each source to “in_manifest: true” when you copy a snapshot into public/data/ and add a manifest entry.

suggestions_next_steps: Add public/data/snapshots/YYYY-MM-DD/kpis.json with machine-readable totals (sales, rent paid, interest, STPL flows) generated by your Python builders.
Keep raw PDF/XLS out of git; commit only CSV/JSON slices with reconciliation_display_source columns preserved.
When CA P&L arrives, add one dataset row and link it from the profit question.
For multi-party trust, version snapshots by date in the filename and repeat the same date in manifest.updated.
